title: 10 best herbal teas for foot odor
intro_desc: Herbal teas for foot odor are infusions made from various plants and herbs that are used to treat foot odor. These teas contain natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help eliminate the bacteria and fungi responsible for foot odor. Some examples of herbal teas that help with foot odor include chamomile, peppermint, sage, and thyme. These teas can be used as foot soaks or compresses to reduce foot odor and improve foot hygiene. By using herbal teas for foot odor, individuals can enjoy the benefits of fresh-smelling feet, improved foot health, and increased confidence in their personal hygiene.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with foot odor because it contains antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can combat the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing unpleasant foot odors. The tea's soothing and anti-inflammatory effects can also help alleviate any discomfort or irritation associated with foot-related issues. By drinking lemon balm tea or using it as a foot soak, the natural compounds found in lemon balm can help eliminate foot odor and promote overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lemon balm tea's antibacterial properties help reduce foot odor by inhibiting the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the feet. This property is important for foot odor as the bacteria breaking down sweat on the feet are the primary cause of the unpleasant smell. The active constituents responsible for this property include citronellal, geraniol, and linalool.

Astringent property: Lemon balm tea has astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce sweating, which can help reduce foot odor. This property is important for foot odor as excessive sweating provides a favorable environment for bacteria to grow and produce foul odors. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins.

Antifungal property: Lemon balm tea's antifungal properties help prevent fungal infections that can cause foot odor. This property is important for foot odor as fungal infections can exacerbate foot odor and lead to other foot problems. The active constituents responsible for this property include citronellal, geraniol, and linalool.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, which can help reduce foot odor. This property is important for foot odor as inflammation and irritation can exacerbate foot odor and lead to other foot problems. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Sedative property: Lemon balm tea's sedative properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which can help reduce foot odor. This property is important for foot odor as stress and anxiety can increase sweating and exacerbate foot odor. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and citronellal.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant have antimicrobial properties that help to kill the bacteria causing foot odor.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids that have a deodorizing effect, making them effective in reducing foot odor.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce sweating and thus preventing foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh lemon balm leaves, rinse, and let dry.
Crush leaves gently and place them in a pot.
Add 4 cups of boiling water to the pot, cover, and steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a foot bath, discarding the leaves.
Soak feet in the lemon balm tea for 15-20 minutes, then pat dry.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with foot odor because it contains antifungal and antibacterial properties that can combat the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The soothing herbal tea made from lavender flowers has a refreshing and calming scent that can leave feet smelling fresh and clean. Drinking lavender tea can also help to reduce inflammation and promote overall foot health. Simply soak your feet in a basin of warm water mixed with lavender tea or add a few drops of lavender essential oil to your foot bath for a relaxing and rejuvenating experience.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: English lavender tea has antibacterial properties that help reduce foot odor by killing the bacteria that cause unpleasant smells. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol.

Antifungal: English lavender tea has antifungal properties that help reduce foot odor by inhibiting the growth of fungi that can cause foot infections and unpleasant smells. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol.

Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce foot odor by reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to unpleasant smells. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol.

Astringent: English lavender tea has astringent properties that help reduce foot odor by constricting the skin's pores and reducing the amount of sweat that bacteria can feed on. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and flavonoids.

Deodorizing: English lavender tea has deodorizing properties that help reduce foot odor by neutralizing unpleasant smells. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, linalyl acetate, and terpinen-4-ol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria and fungi causing foot odor.
Flowers: Have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, while also helping to deodorize and eliminate foot odor.
Buds: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants that can help neutralize foot odor and promote healthy skin.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help reduce sweating and bacteria growth, thereby reducing foot odor.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and essential oils that can help eliminate foot odor and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried English lavender flowers to a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the lavender flowers and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the lavender flowers.
Drink the lavender tea while it's still warm, twice a day for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with foot odor because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can combat the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The thymol compound found in thyme tea inhibits the growth of these microorganisms, thereby reducing foot odor. Additionally, thyme tea has antioxidants that can help eliminate toxins and promote healthy skin. By drinking thyme tea or soaking feet in thyme tea, the natural oils and active compounds can be absorbed through the skin, providing further benefits in managing foot odor.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Thyme tea has strong antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria causing foot odor, thanks to its active constituents like thymol and carvacrol.
Antifungal: Thyme tea's antifungal properties inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for foot odor, as thymol and other compounds present in thyme exhibit fungicidal activities.
Astringent: Thyme tea's astringent properties help control excessive sweating (hyperhidrosis), which contributes to foot odor, due to the tannins present in thyme.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate inflammation and irritation caused by foot odor, as compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids in thyme contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects.

Deodorizing: Thyme tea's deodorizing properties neutralize foot odor by eliminating the bacteria and fungi responsible for the unpleasant smell, attributable to thymol and other volatile oils found in thyme.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antimicrobial compounds that can help eliminate the bacteria causing foot odor.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain potent essential oils that can help neutralize foot odor and leave the feet smelling fresh.
Stems: Thyme stems contain tannins that can help reduce sweating and prevent the growth of odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 tablespoon of dried thyme leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh thyme leaves.
Boil 2 cups of water in a teapot or saucepan.
Steep the thyme leaves in the boiling water for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Soak your feet in the warm thyme tea for 15 minutes, then rinse and dry thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with foot odor because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that can help eliminate the bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor. The tannins present in sage leaves also help to reduce sweating, which can create a moist environment that leads to foot odor. Additionally, sage tea has antioxidants that can help to neutralize the odor caused by bacteria and fungi. Drinking sage tea or soaking your feet in sage tea can help to eliminate foot odor and keep your feet smelling fresh.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Sage tea has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria causing foot odor, thanks to the presence of compounds like eucalyptol and camphor.
Astringent: Sage tea acts as an astringent, helping to reduce perspiration and control foot odor, due to its tannin content.
Fungicidal: Sage tea exhibits fungicidal properties that combat fungal infections causing foot odor, attributable to the essential oils and rosmarinic acid present in sage.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate inflammation and irritation caused by foot odor, owing to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds.
Deodorizing: Sage tea's natural deodorizing properties help neutralize foot odor, as a result of its essential oil content, particularly eucalyptol and camphor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that can help neutralize foot odor caused by bacteria.
Roots: Can have antifungal and antibacterial properties that may help reduce foot odor.
Bark: May contain tannins that can help eliminate foot odor by reducing sweating.
Seeds: Can have antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria causing foot odor.
Rhizomes: May have antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help reduce foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a foot bath, discarding the sage leaves.
Soak feet in the sage tea for 15-20 minutes, then dry thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with foot odor because it contains antibacterial and antifungal properties that can kill the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The rosmarinic acid present in rosemary has potent antibacterial properties that inhibit the growth of odor-causing microorganisms. Moreover, rosemary tea has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and heal any foot infections or irritations. Drinking rosemary tea or using it as a foot soak can help eliminate foot odor, leaving your feet smelling fresh and clean. Additionally, rosemary tea's pleasant aroma can help mask any remaining odor, providing a refreshing and invigorating sensation.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Rosemary tea has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria causing foot odor, thanks to its active constituents like camphor, cineol, and limonene.
Astringent: Rosemary tea's astringent properties help control excess sweat production, which can create a favorable environment for foot odor-causing bacteria, due to its tannins.

Anti-fungal: Rosemary tea has anti-fungal properties that help inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for athlete's foot and other fungal infections causing foot odor, because of its active compounds like eucalyptol and camphene.

Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and soothe irritation caused by foot odor and fungal infections, owing to its rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids.
Deodorizing: Rosemary tea has natural deodorizing properties that help neutralize foot odor, attributed to its volatile oils like camphor, eucalyptol, and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that can help eliminate the bacteria causing foot odor.
Stems: May help reduce sweat production, which can cause foot odor.
Flowers: Can have antibacterial properties that can help neutralize foot odor.
Seeds: May have antifungal properties that can help prevent foot odor.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 4 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the rosemary tea into a foot bath.
Soak your feet in the rosemary tea for 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with foot odor because it contains antibacterial and antifungal properties that can combat the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The tea's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, can eliminate the microorganisms that cause the bad smell. Additionally, oregano tea has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that can alleviate any discomfort or irritation caused by foot odor. Drinking oregano tea or soaking your feet in oregano tea can help eliminate foot odor and keep your feet smelling fresh.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Oregano tea has strong antifungal properties that help combat foot odor by targeting the fungi responsible for athlete's foot and other fungal infections. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.

Antibacterial: Oregano tea has potent antibacterial properties that help reduce foot odor by eliminating the bacteria causing sweaty, smelly feet. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.

Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate foot odor by reducing inflammation and redness associated with bacterial and fungal infections. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid.

Antioxidant: Oregano tea has high antioxidant properties that help combat foot odor by reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall foot health. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid, thymol, and carvacrol.

Astringent: Oregano tea has astringent properties that help reduce foot odor by tightening pores and reducing sweat production. The active constituents responsible for this property are tannins present in oregano.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano tea plant have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing foot odor caused by bacteria and fungi.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help to eliminate foot odor by neutralizing the harmful free radicals that cause unpleasant smells.

Seeds: The seeds of the oregano tea plant contain essential oils that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating foot odor caused by bacterial and fungal infections.
Stems: The stems of the oregano tea plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to reduce foot odor by constricting the skin and reducing sweat production.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, boil 4 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Let the oregano tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak your feet in the oregano tea for 15 minutes.
Rinse your feet with clean water and dry thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tea helps with foot odor because it has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can kill the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The tea's natural oils and compounds, such as citral and geraniol, can inhibit the growth of odor-causing microorganisms and reduce the production of foul-smelling compounds. Additionally, lemongrass tea has a refreshing and invigorating scent that can mask any remaining odor and leave feet feeling fresh and clean. Drinking lemongrass tea can also help detoxify the body and eliminate toxins that can contribute to foot odor. By incorporating lemongrass tea into your foot care routine, you can naturally and effectively combat foot odor and keep your feet smelling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Lemongrass tea contains compounds such as citral and limonene, which exhibit antimicrobial properties that help inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the feet.
Antifungal: The antifungal properties of lemongrass tea, due to the presence of citral and geraniol, help control fungal infections like athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor.
Astringent: Lemongrass tea has astringent properties that help reduce perspiration, creating an environment less conducive to bacterial growth and foot odor.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory effects of lemongrass tea, attributed to citral and citronellal, can help alleviate inflammation and irritation caused by excessive sweating and bacterial activity, reducing foot odor.
Deodorizing: Lemongrass tea's natural deodorizing properties, resulting from its citral and linalool content, neutralize and mask foot odor, providing immediate relief and freshness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemongrass contain essential oils that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing foot odor caused by bacteria and fungi.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of lemongrass have a high concentration of citral, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor.
Stems: The stems of lemongrass contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation caused by foot odor.
Seeds: The seeds of lemongrass contain essential oils that have a strong, refreshing scent, which can help mask the odor caused by foot perspiration.
remedy_recipe: Peel and crush 3-4 lemongrass stalks to release their oils.
Add 4 cups of water to a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the crushed lemongrass stalks to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the lemongrass tea and let it cool down before soaking your feet for 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with foot odor because it contains antimicrobial and antifungal properties that combat the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The tea's main component, eucalyptus oil, has been shown to have potent antibacterial and antifungal effects, making it effective in eliminating the microorganisms that cause foot odor. Furthermore, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and relieve any discomfort or irritation caused by foot odor. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea or using it as a foot soak can help control foot odor and keep feet smelling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Tasmanian blue gum tea has antibacterial properties that help reduce foot odor by killing the bacteria that cause it. The active constituents responsible for its antibacterial properties include terpinen-4-ol, α-pinene, and γ-terpinene.

Antifungal: The antifungal properties of Tasmanian blue gum tea help combat fungal infections causing foot odor. The active constituents responsible for its antifungal properties include 1,8-cineole, α-pinene, and β-pinene.

Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe inflammation and irritation caused by foot odor. The active constituents responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties include α-pinene, limonene, and 1,8-cineole.

Astringent: The astringent properties of Tasmanian blue gum tea help reduce excessive sweating, which can cause foot odor. The active constituents responsible for its astringent properties include tannins and catechins.

Deodorizing: Tasmanian blue gum tea has natural deodorizing properties that help neutralize foot odor. The active constituents responsible for its deodorizing properties include 1,8-cineole, limonene, and α-pinene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant have antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria causing foot odor.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce sweating and preventing foot odor.
Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant have antifungal properties that can help prevent fungal infections and foot odor.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing foot odor.
Seeds: The seeds of the Tasmanian blue gum tea plant contain essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the bacteria causing foot odor.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the leaves, then let it cool until warm.
Soak a clean cloth in the warm tea, then use it to wipe down your feet.
Let the tea dry on your skin before putting on socks or shoes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea helps with foot odor because it contains antibacterial and antifungal properties that can eliminate the bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The tea is made from the bark of the narrow-leaved paperbark tree, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions. The bark contains compounds such as tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils, which have natural antimicrobial properties. By killing the bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor, narrow-leaved paperbark tea can help keep feet smelling fresh and clean. Regularly drinking the tea or using it as a foot soak can provide long-lasting relief from foot odor.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which help inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for breaking down sweat and producing foul-smelling compounds.
Astringent: The tannins present in the tea can help tighten pores and reduce sweat production, thereby limiting the amount of bacteria that can cause foot odor.

Anti-inflammatory: The presence of compounds such as alpha-pinene and limonene in the tea can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, potentially alleviating foot odor caused by skin conditions like athlete's foot.
Fungicidal: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea has fungicidal properties, which can help eliminate fungal infections like athlete's foot that can contribute to foot odor.
Deodorizing: The essential oils in the tea, including alpha-pinene and limonene, can help neutralize odors, providing a temporary solution for foot odor.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the narrow-leaved paperbark tea plant contains oils that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective in reducing foot odor caused by bacteria and fungi.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant have high tannin content, which helps to eliminate the bacteria that cause foot odor, thus providing a natural solution for foot odor problems.
Roots: The roots of the narrow-leaved paperbark tea plant contain active compounds that have antimicrobial properties, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal tea for foot odor.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plant have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them a useful addition to medicinal tea for foot odor.
Flowers: The flowers of the narrow-leaved paperbark tea plant contain volatile oils that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 narrow-leaved paperbark (Melaleuca quinquenervia) leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and contaminants.
Crush leaves gently to release essential oils, then place them in a teapot or infuser.
Pour 1 cup (8 fl. oz.) of boiling water over the crushed leaves and allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the used leaves.
Sip the prepared tea slowly, allowing it to cool before using as a foot soak for 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden geranium tea helps with foot odor because it contains antibacterial and antifungal properties that combat the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the feet. The tea's tannins and essential oils help to eliminate foot odor by closing the pores and reducing perspiration. Furthermore, geranium tea's astringent properties help to tighten and tone the skin, leaving the feet feeling fresh and rejuvenated. By regularly drinking geranium tea and using it as a foot soak, you can effectively manage foot odor and maintain healthy, odor-free feet.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Geranium tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for causing foot odor. The active constituents responsible for this property include geraniol, citronellol, and linalool.
Astringent: Geranium tea helps to constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced and thus reducing foot odor. The tannins present in geranium tea give it this astringent property.

Anti-inflammatory: Geranium tea reduces inflammation and irritation, providing relief from foot odor-related skin issues. The anti-inflammatory properties of geranium tea can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Deodorizing: Geranium tea has a pleasant, sweet, and floral scent that helps to neutralize foot odor. The volatile oils in geranium tea, such as geraniol and citronellol, contribute to its deodorizing properties.
Circulation-boosting: Geranium tea improves blood circulation, which helps to eliminate toxins and reduce foot odor. The essential oils and antioxidants in geranium tea promote better blood flow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the garden geranium tea plant have antibacterial properties that can help reduce foot odor caused by bacteria.
Flowers: The flowers of the garden geranium tea plant contain tannins that can help eliminate the bacteria causing foot odor.
Seeds: The seeds of the garden geranium tea plant contain essential oils that can help neutralize foot odor.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the garden geranium tea plant have antifungal properties that can help prevent foot odor caused by fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh geranium leaves from your herbal garden.
Rinse the geranium leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Coarsely chop the geranium leaves and place them in a pot.
Pour 4 cups of boiling water over the chopped geranium leaves, then let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the geranium tea and discard the leaves; allow the tea to cool before soaking your feet for 15-20 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat foot odor with herbal teas is by soaking your feet in a tea bath. To prepare, steep two to three tea bags in boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes. Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature. Add the tea to a basin of warm water and soak your feet for 15 to 20 minutes. The tannins in the tea help to kill the bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor. Choose tea with strong antibacterial properties, such as black, green, or chamomile tea. Repeat this process regularly for best results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to foot odor that are treated with herbal teas include body odor and bad breath. These conditions can be caused by an imbalance in the body's natural bacteria, which can be restored through the consumption of herbal teas. For example, chamomile tea has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help eliminate the bacteria causing body odor. Peppermint tea can freshen breath by killing the bacteria that cause bad breath. Additionally, sage tea has been shown to be effective in reducing body odor by reducing sweat production. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas as a treatment for these conditions.
